content.text: In English, we use 'a/an' (the indefinite article) and 'the' (the definite article). In Greek, there is no word corresponding to 'a/an', but there is a word for 'the'.

Like the noun, this definite article has number (singular or plural), gender (male, female or neuter), and case (nominative, accusative, genitive or dative). In the first declension, its endings are exactly those of the noun endings.  We will study the first declension noun endings next.
